old_claim.note: Dyed Textiles provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No dyed-textile dependency remains because dye recipes are optional subject matter within the books rather than a requirement for printing or compiling them.

source_shape.source_locator: Description enumerates many recipe categories and the print-history section identifies fifteenth-century Italian editions.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The genre spans medical, domestic, cosmetic, food, ink, stain, and refining instructions rather than one craft lineage.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Mentioning a dyeing application does not make the finished textile category a causal input to the printed collection.

ontology_before: The publication category depended on one of its possible application domains.
ontology_after: Recipe topics are represented in the description while printing remains the direct production dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: chemical_recipe_books no longer directly depends on dyed_textiles.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to a dye-house recipe ledger whose production depended on an operating textile-dyeing practice.
short_reason: Dyeing is content in the book, not its prerequisite.
